issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened or closed, no ARIA live region is updated to announce the change. Users of screen readers receive no feedback that the menu has expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: After `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');`. After closing, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: Without `aria‑controls`, assistive technologies cannot associate the toggle with the pricing comparison table it shows/hides.
issuesFound.fix: If the comparison table has an id (e.g., `pricing‑comparison`), add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-controls', 'pricing‑comparison');`.

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: Keyboard‑only users cannot discover the purpose of the button.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_btn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.scrollToTop || 'Scroll back to top');` or provide visible text inside the button.

issuesFound.title: Search clear button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: Screen‑reader users cannot identify the button’s purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_clearBtn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.clearSearch || 'Clear search');`.

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher toggle buttons missing `aria‑pressed` state on init
issuesFound.explanation: Assistive technologies cannot tell which theme is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: After reading the stored theme, set `setAttr(btn, 'aria‑pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data‑theme‑toggle') === currentTheme));`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay element missing `role="dialog"` and `aria‑modal`
issuesFound.explanation: Screen‑reader users are not informed that an off‑canvas menu has opened.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_overlay, 'role', 'dialog'); setAttr(_overlay, 'aria-modal', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels lack `aria‑hidden` when collapsed (rely only on `hidden` attribute)
issuesFound.explanation: Some AT may still expose the content.
issuesFound.fix: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` also call `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison table rows missing `role="row"` and cells missing `role="gridcell"`
issuesFound.explanation: Users of assistive technologies may have difficulty navigating the table.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(row, 'role', 'row');` to each `<tr>` and `setAttr(cell, 'role', 'gridcell');` to each `<td>`/`<th>`.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hides via CSS class without removing it from the accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `stickyHeader.hide()` method adds the CSS class `headerHidden` to the `<header>` element but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` or the `hidden` attribute, leaving the hidden header still perceivable by assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hide/show actions are not announced to screen‑reader users
issuesFound.explanation: The `stickyHeader.show()` and `stickyHeader.hide()` methods change visual state but never provide an ARIA live‑region announcement, so users are unaware when the header appears or disappears.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` functions toggle visibility of dropdown panels but do not write to any `role="status"`/`role="alert"` element, leaving users without an audible cue that a menu has opened or closed.

issuesFound.title: Simple pricing “show/hide” toggle button is missing `aria-controls` for the region it toggles
issuesFound.explanation: The element `[data-pricing-toggle]` controls the visibility of a region (`_drawer`) but only sets `aria-checked`; it does not reference the target region with `aria-controls`.

issuesFound.title: Simple pricing toggle button does not use `aria-expanded` to indicate the expanded/collapsed state of the controlled region
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle uses `aria-checked` to reflect on/off state but does not set `aria-expanded`, which is the appropriate attribute for a control that shows or hides a region.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not set `aria-expanded` to reflect the expanded/collapsed state of the price list
issuesFound.explanation: The period buttons use `aria-pressed` to indicate selection but do not convey that the price list region is being expanded/changed via `aria-expanded`.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel navigation buttons lack `aria-controls` to identify the carousel they operate on
issuesFound.explanation: The previous/next buttons (`[data-carousel-prev]`, `[data-carousel-next]`) control the carousel slides but do not have `aria-controls` referencing the slide container, making it unclear to assistive technologies which element is being manipulated.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel navigation buttons do not announce slide changes via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the carousel moves to the next or previous slide, no ARIA live‑region update is issued, so screen‑reader users receive no feedback about the new slide.

Testimonials carousel navigation buttons lack `aria-controls` to identify the carousel they operate on
Testimonials carousel navigation buttons do not announce slide changes via a live region

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is moved off‑screen with CSS (`removeClass(_drawer, …)`) while remaining in the DOM, so screen‑reader users can still navigate to its hidden content.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.close`, after hiding the drawer add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `mobileNav.open` remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing `aria-controls` link to drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button does not reference the navigation drawer, breaking the relationship required for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer has an `id` (e.g., `setAttr(_drawer, 'id', 'mobile-nav-drawer')`) and set `aria-controls="mobile-nav-drawer"` on the toggle button.

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan cards do not announce price changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, the numeric price values are updated visually but no `aria-live` region announces the new amounts, leaving AT users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After updating each price element, also set `priceDisplay.textContent = formatted; liveRegion.announce(`${plan.id} price updated to ${formatted}`);`.

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle button missing an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The switch that toggles monthly/annual billing has no label, so its purpose is unclear to AT users.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.toggleBilling || 'Toggle billing period')`.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hidden via CSS class without `aria-hidden` when scrolled out of view
issuesFound.explanation: The header is moved off‑screen with CSS but remains in the accessibility tree, causing screen‑reader users to encounter hidden navigation links.
issuesFound.fix: When adding the hidden class, also set `setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; remove the attribute when showing the header.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels lack explicit ARIA role `menu` or `region` for screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Without a role, assistive technologies cannot convey the purpose of the panel.
issuesFound.fix: In `register`, add `setAttr(panel, 'role', 'menu')` (or `region` if appropriate).

issuesFound.title: Honeypot input not hidden from assistive technologies
issuesFound.explanation: The hidden field may be read by screen readers, causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(input, 'aria-hidden', 'true')` when creating the honeypot element.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay uses `hidden` attribute but lacks `aria-hidden` when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Although `hidden` removes the element from the accessibility tree, adding `aria-hidden` improves consistency and future‑proofing.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_overlay, 'aria-hidden', 'true')` when hidden and remove it when shown.

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison table toggle button missing `aria-controls` link to the table
issuesFound.explanation: Assistive technologies cannot associate the button with the table it expands/collapses.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the trigger button has `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', table.id)` and update `aria-expanded` accordingly.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance does not set aria-expanded="true" on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance` the panel is shown and CSS classes are added, but the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never set to “true”. Screen readers will think the menu remains collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria-expanded="true" on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open` expands the answer panel and animates it, but does not update the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute to “true”. Users of assistive technology cannot perceive the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the beginning of the `open` function.

issuesFound.title: Site search suggestion fetch updates UI without announcing changes via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_fetchAndRender` populates the suggestion list and updates the status text, but the status element lacks `aria-live` and no live‑region announcement is made. Users are not informed that new suggestions have loaded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_statusEl, 'aria-live', 'polite');` (or use `liveRegion.announce`) after updating the status text.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator._renderResult` marks fields as valid/invalid by adding CSS classes, but it never sets or clears the `aria-invalid` attribute. Assistive technologies cannot detect validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: In `_renderResult`, call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', result.valid ? 'false' : 'true');` (and remove it when valid).

issuesFound.title: FAQ items are missing initial aria-expanded="false" on their triggers
issuesFound.explanation: `faqRegistry.parse` only sets `aria-expanded` to “true” when the item is initially open; otherwise the attribute is omitted. All collapsible triggers must have an explicit `aria-expanded` state.
issuesFound.fix: After creating the trigger, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when `isInitiallyOpen` is false.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close function hides drawer via CSS without setting aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.close` removes CSS classes to hide the drawer but never sets `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer element. The drawer remains in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` in the `close` function after the transition.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu opening does not announce the action to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened (`_openInstance`), no live‑region announcement is made. Users relying on AT receive no feedback that the menu expanded.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu expanded');` (or a more specific label).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close does not set aria-hidden="true" on the panel when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.close` animates the panel closed and adds the `hidden` attribute, but it never sets `aria-hidden="true"`. The panel stays perceivable to AT.
issuesFound.fix: After the animation completes, add `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not remove aria-hidden from the panel when shown
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open` removes the `hidden` attribute and animates the panel, but does not clear `aria-hidden`. The panel may still be hidden from assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: In `open`, after making the panel visible, call `item.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');`.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation close function hides drawer via CSS without setting aria-hidden
FAQ accordion open does not remove aria-hidden from the panel when shown

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance does not set aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: _dropdownMenu._openInstance() adds the “open” class to the panel but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the dropdown is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside _openInstance().

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() expands an FAQ item’s panel but does not set aria‑expanded="true" on the associated trigger button, breaking the ARIA accordion pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when opening the item.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate() returns validation results but never applies aria‑invalid="true" to fields that fail validation, so AT cannot convey error state.
issuesFound.fix: In _inlineValidator._renderResult() (or equivalent), call field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); when result.valid is false.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, inlineValidator removes error text but does not remove aria‑invalid, leaving the invalid state lingering for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In _inlineValidator._renderResult() when result.valid is true, call field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');.

issuesFound.title: FAQ trigger elements missing initial aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: During FAQ parsing, trigger buttons are given aria-controls but are not given an initial aria‑expanded state (default should be “false”). This violates the ARIA accordion pattern on page load.
issuesFound.fix: In faqRegistry.parse(), after creating trigger, add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); unless the item is meant to be open by default.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email field validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The email <input> in newsletterForm uses inlineValidator for validation, but the validator never applies aria‑invalid on error, so screen readers cannot detect the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the inlineValidator binding for the email field sets aria‑invalid when validation fails (see Issue 4).

issuesFound.title: Contact form fields validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: contactForm binds its inputs to inlineValidator, which does not set aria‑invalid on errors, leaving all fields without proper AT error indication.
issuesFound.fix: Apply the same fix as Issue 4 to each field bound in contactForm (e.g., setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true') on validation failure).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger may lack aria‑controls when drawer has no id
issuesFound.explanation: In mobileNav.init() the trigger receives aria-controls only if _navDrawer.id exists. If the drawer element lacks an id, the trigger has no aria‑controls, breaking the relationship between control and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer element always has a unique id (e.g., _navDrawer.id = 'nav-drawer';) or always set aria-controls on the trigger to that id.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger may lack aria‑controls when panel has no id
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu.register() adds aria-controls to the trigger only if the panel already has an id. If the panel lacks an id, the trigger has no aria-controls, violating the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Generate a unique id for the panel when missing (e.g., panel.id = srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel');) before setting aria-controls on the trigger.

Contact form fields validation does not set aria‑invalid
Mobile navigation trigger may lack aria‑controls when drawer has no id
Dropdown menu trigger may lack aria‑controls when panel has no id

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria-expanded update on open
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_openInstance` in the dropdown menu controller adds the “open” class and removes the `hidden` attribute from the dropdown panel, but it never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. This leaves screen‑reader users unaware that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after opening the panel.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria-expanded update on open (second instance)
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 1 for each additional dropdown trigger created by `register`. Each trigger must receive `aria-expanded="true"` when its panel opens.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure `_openInstance` updates `aria-expanded` for the specific trigger element.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.open` adds CSS classes and shows the drawer but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Only the close function sets `aria-expanded="false"`. Users of assistive technologies cannot tell when the navigation is open.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the `open` function.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email field does not receive aria-invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: Validation of the newsletter email field uses `inlineValidator`, which only adds an error message element. It never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the `<input>` when the value is invalid, so assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator._renderResult`, when `result.valid` is false, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Full name” field missing aria-invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: The “Full name” input is validated but the error handling does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field, leaving the error invisible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator._renderResult`, set `aria-invalid="true"` for invalid fields.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Email address” field missing aria-invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 6 for the email input; no `aria-invalid` is applied on validation failure.
issuesFound.fix: Apply `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` when validation fails.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Subject” field missing aria-invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: The subject input lacks `aria-invalid` when validation fails.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` on error.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Message” textarea missing aria-invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea does not receive `aria-invalid="true"` on validation error, preventing screen‑reader users from perceiving the problem.
issuesFound.fix: Set `aria-invalid="true"` when the message fails validation.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Privacy consent” checkbox missing aria-invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: The required consent checkbox is validated, but when unchecked the field is not marked with `aria-invalid="true"`.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` is applied for invalid consent.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria-controls attribute on init (first instance)
issuesFound.explanation: In `register`, the trigger button receives `aria-controls` only if the panel already has an `id`. If the panel lacks an `id`, the attribute is not added, breaking the relationship for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Generate an `id` for the panel when missing (`srUtils.ensureId(panel)`) and always set `aria-controls` on the trigger.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria-controls attribute on init (subsequent instances)
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 11 for each additional dropdown registered. Ensure every trigger has a matching `aria-controls`.
issuesFound.fix: Apply the same fix as Issue 11 for all registrations.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger (".nav-toggle") is used to open and close the off‑canvas drawer, but no aria‑expanded attribute is set on page load. Screen readers cannot know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init, add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') and ensure the attribute is toggled in the open and close functions.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden via CSS only, no aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer element is shown/hidden by adding/removing CSS classes (e.g., is-open) without setting aria‑hidden. When hidden, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open set setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false'); and in mobileNav.close set setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true') or use setAttribute('hidden').

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The product filter updates the visible count badge (data‑product‑count) but does not provide an aria‑live region or other announcement. Users of assistive technology are unaware of the number of matching products.
issuesFound.fix: Add an off‑screen element with aria‑live="polite" near the count badge and update its text whenever the count changes, or set aria‑label on the badge with the new count.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Inline validation (e.g., newsletterForm, contactForm) marks fields as invalid visually but never sets aria‑invalid="true" on the input, nor removes it when the field becomes valid. Assistive technologies cannot convey error state.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator.validate, after determining validity call field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', result.valid ? 'false' : 'true') and remove the attribute when valid.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel slides hidden via transform without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Slides are moved off‑screen with CSS transforms to hide them, but no aria‑hidden attribute is applied. Hidden slides remain in the accessibility tree, causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix: In testimonialsCarousel._reveal, set setAttr(slide, 'aria-hidden', 'false') for the active slide and setAttr(otherSlide, 'aria-hidden', 'true') for all others.

issuesFound.title: Product filter applying filters does not announce changes via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the UI updates the product grid and count badge, but no live‑region announcement is made. Users of screen readers are not informed that the results have changed.
issuesFound.fix: After productFilter.applyFilters executes, call liveRegion.announce(`Showing ${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''}`) or similar.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger opens the drawer with id “main‑nav” (or similar) but the button does not have an aria‑controls attribute referencing that drawer. Assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init, after locating the drawer element, add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id) (if the drawer has an id) or generate one.

issuesFound.title: Search status element not exposed to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The search component creates a status element with aria‑live="polite", but it is appended after the input without an associated aria‑describedby reference, so screen readers may not read updates.
issuesFound.fix: Set setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-describedby', srUtils.ensureId(_statusEl)) so the live region is linked to the input.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels lack role="menu" or appropriate ARIA labeling
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panels are identified only by CSS classes and hidden attributes; they do not have role="menu" or aria‑label/aria‑labelledby, making navigation unclear for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.register, after locating the panel, add setAttr(panel, 'role', 'menu') and, if a label exists, set aria‑labelledby to the trigger’s id.

fpTitles: Testimonials carousel slides hidden via transform without aria‑hidden
Search status element not exposed to assistive technology

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu open/close functions change the visibility of dropdown panels but never update the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed attribute
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that act as binary toggles (e.g., play/pause, mute/unmute) are not updated with aria‑pressed, so assistive technologies cannot convey their state.
issuesFound.fix: In each toggle handler (e.g., mediaPlayerController _onKey for the “m” key), add `setAttr(media, 'aria-pressed', String(media.muted))` or similar to reflect the pressed state.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: siteSearch._updateStatus changes the status text for search results but does not send an aria‑live announcement, so users are not informed of result counts or errors.
issuesFound.fix: After setting `_statusEl.textContent`, call `liveRegion.announce(_statusEl.textContent)` (or `announceAssertive` for errors).

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate marks fields as invalid visually but only adds aria‑describedby; it never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the input, violating WCAG 2.1.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator._renderResult, when `!result.valid`, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; when valid, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: After a field becomes valid, inlineValidator only removes error styling but does not remove `aria-invalid`, leaving the input incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator._renderResult, when `result.valid`, call `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid')`.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialized on page load for mobile navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button lacks an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, so its collapsed state is not conveyed to assistive technologies on load.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init, after locating `_trigger`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger does not have `aria-controls` pointing to the navigation drawer’s id, breaking the relationship between control and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init, ensure the drawer has an id (e.g., `srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer')`) and set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id)`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open and close change panel visibility but do not provide an aria‑live announcement, leaving users unaware of which question is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened?.replace('{title}', item.question) ?? 'Answer expanded')` for open and a similar message for close.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce update
issuesFound.explanation: pricing.setPeriod updates plan prices but does not announce the change, so users of assistive technology may miss the new pricing information.
issuesFound.fix: After updating all plan cards, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged?.replace('{period}', period) ?? 'Pricing updated to ' + period)`.

issuesFound.title: User actions that change visible content lack dedicated live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Several interactive handlers (e.g., navKeyboardShortcuts shortcuts, productFilter.applyFilters updating the product count badge) modify visible text without writing to a dedicated aria‑live region, violating WCAG 2.1.4.
issuesFound.fix: After each visible update, write the new text to an element with `role="status"` or `aria-live="polite"` (e.g., a hidden live region) and call `liveRegion.announce` with the same message.

fpTitles: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
User actions that change visible content lack dedicated live‑region announcements

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel play/pause button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The button with `data-carousel-play` toggles automatic slide rotation via `togglePlay()`, but the script never updates `aria‑pressed` (or any other state attribute) on the button. Assistive technologies cannot tell whether the carousel is currently playing or paused.
issuesFound.fix:
In `togglePlay()` (or the click handler) add `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', String(!_playBtn.getAttribute('aria-pressed') === 'true'));` and ensure the initial state is set (`aria-pressed="true"` when playing, `"false"` when paused).

---

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input validation errors are not reflected in aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form binds the email field with `inlineValidator.bind(_emailEl, ['required', 'email']);`. `inlineValidator` marks errors by adding CSS classes only; it never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the input, nor removes it on success. This violates WCAG 2.1 A.
issuesFound.fix:
In `inlineValidator._renderResult` (or by extending the validator) add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', String(!result.valid));` when the field is invalid and remove the attribute when valid.

---

issuesFound.title: Contact‑form fields validation errors are not reflected in aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form uses `inlineValidator.bind(field, cfg.rules);` for each field, but the validator does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the inputs when they fail validation. Users of assistive technology receive no programmatic indication of the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
Extend the inline‑validation rendering to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field when `result.valid` is false and remove the attribute when the field becomes valid, mirroring the fix in Issue 3.

---

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hidden via CSS class does not receive aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: In **stickyHeader**, when the user scrolls down the header is hidden by adding the CSS class `headerHidden`. The element remains in the accessibility tree because no `aria-hidden` attribute is applied, causing screen readers to still announce the hidden header.
issuesFound.fix:
In the branch that adds `headerHidden` (`if (movingDown) { … addClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden); … }`) also call `setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. When the header is shown again, remove the attribute (`removeAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden');`).

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not manage aria‑hidden when opened/closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer element (`_drawer`) is shown/hidden by toggling CSS classes (`is-open`) but the script never sets `aria-hidden` on the drawer. When the drawer is hidden it remains perceivable to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
In `open()` after adding the open class, call `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');`. In `close()` after removing the open class, call `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Search‑results highlighter updates page content without a live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: `searchResultsHighlighter.highlight()` injects `<mark>` elements into the DOM to highlight query terms, but no live‑region is used to announce that the page content has changed. Users of screen readers are unaware that new markup has been added.
issuesFound.fix:
After performing the highlight, update a hidden live‑region (e.g., an element with `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"`) with a brief message such as “Search terms highlighted”. Example: `liveRegion.announce('Search terms have been highlighted');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button does not expose its state via aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle (`_toggleEl`) is a custom switch that toggles between monthly and annual plans. The script updates `aria-checked` but does not set `aria-pressed`, which is the expected state attribute for a toggle button. Assistive technologies may not convey the current state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_onToggleClick()` (or after changing the period) add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));` and ensure the initial markup includes `aria-pressed="false"`.

---

issuesFound.title: View‑mode toggle buttons do not set aria‑pressed consistently on initial load
issuesFound.explanation: The view‑mode buttons (`data-view-mode`) are given `aria-pressed` only when the user clicks them. On page load the button representing the persisted view mode (`grid` or `list`) may lack the correct `aria-pressed` value, leaving the current mode ambiguous to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
After reading the persisted view mode from storage, call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === persistedMode));` for each button during initialization.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger elements lack explicit `aria-controls` linking to their panels when the panel ID is generated dynamically
issuesFound.explanation: In **dropdownMenu.register()**, if the trigger does not already have an `aria-controls` attribute, the script generates a panel ID (`panelId = srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel');`) and sets `aria-controls` on the trigger. However, when the panel already has an existing ID, the generated ID is not used, leaving the trigger’s `aria-controls` pointing to a non‑existent element.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure that when the panel already has an ID, the trigger’s `aria-controls` is set to that exact ID: replace the block with `const panelId = panel.id || srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel'); setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);`. This guarantees a valid reference.

fpTitles: Contact‑form fields validation errors are not reflected in aria‑invalid

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not set aria‑expanded on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions add/remove CSS classes and lock scrolling, but never update aria‑expanded on the hamburger button (`.nav-toggle`). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the navigation drawer is open.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1152: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`  
    Line 1174: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria‑controls on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger (`.nav-toggle`) controls the drawer (`#nav-drawer`) but never receives an aria‑controls reference, breaking the relationship for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1150: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));`  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded when opened/closed
issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu._onTriggerClick() the button’s visual state toggles, but the code never sets aria‑expanded to true or false on the trigger element, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1408 (open case): `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`  
    Line 1412 (close case): `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “open” function does not set aria‑expanded to true on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() adds CSS classes and animates the panel, but never updates aria‑expanded on the associated trigger, so the expanded state is not conveyed to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2104: `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`  

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not set aria‑expanded on the period buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The period buttons (`[data-period]`) change the displayed prices, but the script never updates aria‑expanded to reflect the selected state, breaking the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2502 (inside setPeriod() when iterating buttons): `setAttr(btn, ARIA.EXPANDED, btnPeriod === period ? 'true' : 'false');`  

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change is not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, the UI updates prices but no live‑region announcement is made, leaving users unaware of the new pricing context.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2510 (after updating all cards): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || \`Billing period switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}\`);`  

---

issuesFound.title: Inline field validator never sets aria‑invalid on invalid inputs
issuesFound.explanation:
inlineValidator.validate() adds an error message element and CSS classes, but it only calls srUtils.markInvalid() which adds aria‑describedby — it never sets aria‑invalid="true". Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect the error state.  
  Fix (both locations):  
    After detecting an invalid result, add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`  
    When the field becomes valid, remove the attribute (`field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`).  

---

issuesFound.title: Product filter updates (result count, empty‑state) are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible product count badge changes and an empty‑state message may appear, but no live‑region is used to announce these changes. Users of screen readers receive no feedback about how many products match.
issuesFound.fix:
After _updateCountBadge(count) add: `liveRegion.announce(\`\${count} product\${count===1?'':'s'} found\`);`  
    After _updateEmptyState(count) if count===0 add: `liveRegion.announce('No products match your filters');`  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the off‑canvas navigation updates visual state but does not provide an audible cue. Screen‑reader users cannot know when the menu becomes available or hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
In open() after addClass(_drawer,…): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`  
    In close() after removeClass(_drawer,…): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle (`_toggleEl` in the pricing module) changes visual state and the selected period but never updates `aria‑pressed` on the element, so assistive technologies cannot tell whether the switch is on or off.
issuesFound.fix: In the `_onToggleClick` (and `_onToggleKeydown`) handlers call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(_toggleEl.getAttribute('aria-pressed') !== 'true'));` after toggling the period.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields (newsletter email)
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.bind()` validates fields but only adds error messages; it never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to the field when validation fails, so screen readers do not receive the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator.validate()` after a failed validation, call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` (or use `srUtils.markInvalid` which also sets the attribute).

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields (contact form “Full name”)
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 3, affecting the contact form’s “Full name” input (`[name="full_name"]`). The field receives an error message but no `aria-invalid` attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the validation routine adds `aria-invalid="true"` to the field when the “Full name” validation fails.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields (contact form “Email address”)
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form’s email input (`[name="email"]`) is validated but never receives `aria-invalid="true"` on error.
issuesFound.fix: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` when the email validation fails.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields (contact form “Message”)
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea (`[name="message"]`) is validated but does not get `aria-invalid="true"` when the content is too short or too long.
issuesFound.fix: Apply `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` on validation failure.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not clear aria‑invalid on valid fields (newsletter email)
issuesFound.explanation: After a previously invalid newsletter email field becomes valid, `aria-invalid` remains set because the validator only removes the error message.
issuesFound.fix: In the success branch of `inlineValidator.validate()`, call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` (or set it to `"false"`).

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not clear aria‑invalid on valid fields (contact form “Full name”)
issuesFound.explanation: When the “Full name” field becomes valid, the lingering `aria-invalid` attribute is not removed.
issuesFound.fix: Remove or reset `aria-invalid` in the successful validation path for that field.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not clear aria‑invalid on valid fields (contact form “Email address”)
issuesFound.explanation: The email input retains `aria-invalid="true"` after the user corrects the address.
issuesFound.fix: Clear the attribute when validation passes.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the product grid is filtered, the visible count badge (`[data-product-count]`) is updated, but no ARIA live region is used to announce the new count to screen‑reader users, leaving them unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the badge text in `productFilter._updateCountBadge()`, also set `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count===1?'':'s'} shown`);` or add an off‑screen live region that receives the count.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not update aria‑expanded on trigger button (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The openInstance and closeInstance functions in the dropdown menu controller toggle visibility of the panel but never set aria‑expanded on the trigger element, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: After changing the panel’s visibility, call setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') when opening and setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') when closing.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls attribute (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button controls the navigation drawer but does not reference the drawer via aria‑controls, breaking the relationship for AT.
issuesFound.fix: After obtaining the drawer element, ensure setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', drawer.id) (where drawer.id is generated if absent).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden via CSS class only, without aria‑hidden ( MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close removes the is‑open class but does not set aria‑hidden="true" on the drawer, so the element remains in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix: In close add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and in open remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Inline field validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid inputs (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate marks fields as invalid visually but only adds aria‑describedby via srUtils.markInvalid; it never sets aria‑invalid="true", violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In _ renderResult when result.valid is false, call setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and when valid, remove or set to 'false'.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error state not cleared aria‑invalid on valid input (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the validator removes visual error classes but does not remove aria‑invalid, leaving the element incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In _ renderResult for the valid branch, call removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid') or setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Product filter UI updates (count badge, empty‑state message, pagination) are not announced via a live region (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible count badge and empty‑state visibility change, but no aria‑live region is updated, so users of assistive technology receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix: Add a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> element (e.g., #filter-status) and update its text with the result count after each filter operation.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content changes in FAQ accordion (panel open/close) are not announced via live region (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing an FAQ item changes visible content but does not write to an aria‑live region, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, call liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened or STRINGS.faqClosed) with the question title.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestions list updates are not announced via live region (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The number of suggestions changes as the user types, but only a hidden status element is updated; however, the live region is not used to announce the count, so AT users may not know results are available.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure _ statusEl has role="status" and aria‑live="polite", and update its text with the suggestion count; optionally call liveRegion.announce with a concise message.

fpTitles: Form validation error state not cleared aria‑invalid on valid input (MEDIUM)

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input missing aria-describedby linking to error message
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput for the newsletter email has an aria-describedby attribute referencing “newsletter-email-error”, but no element with that id is rendered, so screen readers cannot hear error details. This affects users relying on AT to understand validation errors.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Full name” input missing aria-describedby linking to error message
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput for “Full name” includes aria-describedby pointing to “contact-name-error”, but no element with that id exists, preventing AT users from hearing the error message. Affects users with visual impairments.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Email address” input missing aria-describedby linking to error message
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput for “Email address” references “contact-email-error” via aria-describedby, but no element with that id is rendered, so validation errors are not announced to AT users.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Subject” input missing aria-describedby linking to error message
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput for “Subject” includes aria-describedby pointing to “contact-subject-error”, but no element with that id exists, so AT users cannot hear the error message.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Message” textarea missing aria-describedby linking to error message
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea for “Message” references “contact-message-error” via aria-describedby, but no element with that id is rendered, preventing AT users from hearing validation errors.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter submit button missing aria-busy for loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button used for newsletter submission receives a “loading” prop (which the Button component does not recognize) instead of the required “isLoading”. Consequently aria-busy is never set, so assistive technologies are not informed of the loading state. Affects users waiting for submission feedback.

issuesFound.title: Contact form submit button missing aria-busy for loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button in the contact form also uses an unrecognized “loading” prop instead of “isLoading”, so aria-busy is not applied and AT users are not notified when the form is submitting. This hinders users awaiting confirmation.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input aria-describedby points to non‑existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput’s aria-describedby references “newsletter-email-error”, but no element with that id is ever rendered, breaking the relationship and confusing AT users.

issuesFound.title: Contact form field aria-describedby points to non‑existent error IDs
issuesFound.explanation: Each TextInput/textarea in the contact form references an error ID (e.g., “contact-name-error”) via aria-describedby, but the corresponding <ErrorMessage> elements are never rendered because the FormGroup is passed an undefined errorId prop. This breaks the relationship.
issuesFound.fix:
Update each FormGroup usage to pass the correct errorId and render the error message, e.g.:

Line 1392 (ContactForm name field):
```tsx
<FormGroup
  label="Full name"
  htmlFor="contact-name"
  required
  errorId="contact-name-error"
  errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}
>
  <TextInput
    id="contact-name"
    name="name"
    type="text"
    autoComplete="name"
    required
    aria-required="true"
    value={name.state.value}
    onChange={(ev) => name.onChange(ev.target.value)}
    onBlur={name.onBlur}
    invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error}
    aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined}
  />
</FormGroup>
```
Apply analogous changes for email, subject, and message fields, ensuring each errorId matches the rendered <ErrorMessage> id.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, rendering the focus ring invisible for keyboard users. outline-offset alone does not create a visible indicator. This blocks keyboard navigation access.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible rule inside @media (forced-colors: active) sets outline: none with only outline-offset. High-contrast mode users will not see a focus indicator, blocking access in this mode.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset. Buttons are critical interactive elements; removing the visible focus ring blocks keyboard access to all buttons using this class.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset. This hides the focus ring for the mobile navigation toggle, blocking keyboard access to the menu.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links have outline: none with only outline-offset on focus. Keyboard users cannot identify which submenu link is focused, blocking navigation within dropdowns.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible rule overrides the valid :focus style with outline: none and no replacement. Keyboard users typing in the search field will not see a focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has outline: none with only outline-offset on focus. Keyboard users cannot identify the button as focused, blocking form submission via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links have outline: none with only outline-offset on focus. Keyboard users cannot identify focused links in the account menu, blocking access to account features.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). Users with motor impairments may fail to activate small buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon buttons are often critical controls; small sizes increase error rates for touch users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is explicitly sized to 20px × 20px, below the 24px minimum. While the label may be larger, the input itself should meet the minimum size for direct interaction.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes content fully visible instead of hiding it visually. This breaks the utility intended for screen-reader-only text, exposing it to sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty and does not disable the transform transition on .skip-link (Line 433). Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the slide animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block contains empty rules for .hero-image img and does not disable the 3D transform transition (Line 763). Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the rotation animation.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses words together. This significantly reduces readability for users with cognitive or visual impairments.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing: -0.05em, which compresses characters. This reduces legibility, especially for users with low vision or dyslexia.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .feature-card p
issuesFound.explanation: The text color var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on background var(--colour-bg-page) (#ffffff) has a contrast ratio of ~2.7:1, failing the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text. Users with low vision may not read this content.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .input-hint
issuesFound.explanation: The text color var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on background var(--colour-bg-page) (#ffffff) has a contrast ratio of ~2.7:1. Helper text is critical for form completion; low contrast makes it inaccessible.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing a non-colour alternative (like bold or border) in the default state. Links are distinguished only by colour, failing WCAG 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller updates aria-expanded on the trigger and toggles the hidden attribute on the panel, but it does not write a status message to an aria-live region (e.g. "Menu opened" or "Menu closed"). Screen reader users may miss the state change if they are not focused on the trigger element when it occurs.
issuesFound.fix: Line N: liveRegion.announce(inst.open ? 'Menu opened' : 'Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent actions not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the user accepts or declines cookies, the banner is hidden but no confirmation message is sent to an aria-live region. Users relying on screen readers are not informed that their preference was saved or that the dialog has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line N: liveRegion.announce(decision === 'accepted' ? 'Cookie preferences accepted.' : 'Cookie preferences declined.');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not removed from accessibility tree on close
issuesFound.explanation: The close function removes the open CSS class but does not explicitly set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element. If the CSS closed state does not use display: none (e.g. uses transform), the drawer remains in the accessibility tree and can be navigated to by screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line N: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers lack keyboard activation for Enter/Space
issuesFound.explanation: The keydown handler supports arrow keys for navigation between triggers, but it does not handle Enter or Space keys to activate (toggle) the focused trigger. This makes the accordion inaccessible to keyboard users if the trigger is a non-native button (e.g. div[role="button"]).
issuesFound.fix: Line N: case KEYS.ENTER: case KEYS.SPACE: e.preventDefault(); toggle(item); break;

issuesFound.title: Stat counter animations not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The numeric counter updates its text content dynamically via JavaScript animation, but the element does not have aria-live="polite" and no announcement is made. Screen reader users will not hear the numbers counting up or the final value.
issuesFound.fix: Line N: el.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');

issuesFound.title: File upload validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a file fails validation (e.g. wrong type or size), an error message is announced via live region, but aria-invalid="true" is not set on the associated input element. This prevents screen readers from identifying the specific field as invalid during form validation summaries.
issuesFound.fix: Line N: binding.inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Discount code input validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When an invalid discount code is entered, the status message is updated, but aria-invalid="true" is not set on the input element itself. Assistive technologies may not associate the error state with the input field.
issuesFound.fix: Line N: _inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel play/pause state not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Toggling the auto-play state updates the button label and aria-pressed attribute, but no message is sent to an aria-live region. Users are not explicitly informed that auto-rotation has started or stopped.
issuesFound.fix: Line N: liveRegion.announce(_playing ? 'Automatic slide rotation started.' : 'Automatic slide rotation paused.');

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher lacks keyboard support for non-button toggles
issuesFound.explanation: The theme switcher attaches a click listener to [data-theme-toggle] elements via document delegation. If these elements are not native buttons (e.g. divs), they will not respond to Enter or Space keys, making them inaccessible to keyboard-only users.
issuesFound.fix: Line N: document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.target.matches('[data-theme-toggle]') && (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ')) { e.preventDefault(); e.target.click(); } });

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions in `dropdownMenu` update the visual state and `aria-expanded` attribute but do not announce the state change (open/close) to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on assistive technology are not informed when a dropdown menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1656: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');
  Line 1670: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in `mobileNav` toggle the CSS class `is-open` on the `_drawer` element but do not explicitly toggle the `aria-hidden` attribute. If the drawer is hidden via CSS properties like `visibility` or `transform` rather than `display: none`, it remains accessible to screen readers when closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1386: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');
  Line 1410: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Viewport reveal elements not managed with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `_reveal` function in `viewportReveal` adds the `is-revealed` class to elements but does not manage the `aria-hidden` attribute. If the initial hidden state is achieved via `visibility: hidden` or `opacity: 0` (common for reveal animations), the elements remain in the accessibility tree before revelation, causing confusion for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3565: el.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Discount code validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onApply` function in `discountCodeInput` validates the input and displays an error message if the code is invalid, but it does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the `_inputEl`. Screen reader users are not explicitly informed that the input field itself is in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3195: srUtils.markInvalid(_inputEl, _statusEl);

issuesFound.title: File upload zone missing aria-controls reference to file list
issuesFound.explanation: The `bind` function in `fileUploadField` sets `role="button"` and `aria-label` on the `zoneEl` but does not set `aria-controls` to reference the `listEl` id. This breaks the programmatic relationship between the upload trigger and the list of selected files.

Screen reader users are not informed when a dropdown menu opens or closes.

The close() function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state where screen readers are not informed when an answer expands.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid/markValid functions missing aria-invalid attribute

Form fields validated by inlineValidator (used in contactForm, newsletterForm, multiStepWizard) will not announce their invalid state to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement

While focus movement helps, an explicit announcement confirms the action to screen reader users, especially if scroll behavior is smooth and delayed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
