issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open() function adds CSS classes and animates the panel but never explicitly sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Only the close() function sets aria-expanded="false". Screen readers will not receive the expanded state update when opening FAQ items.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2831-2843: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); after item.open = true; in the open() function

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close function uses string literal instead of ARIA constant
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function uses setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') with a string literal instead of the ARIA.EXPANDED constant defined at line 285. This creates inconsistency and potential for typos across the codebase.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2847: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion init does not set initial aria-expanded states before DOM ready
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion init() function applies aria-expanded states during initialization but there is no guarantee this runs before users can interact with FAQ items. If a user tabs to a trigger before init completes, the aria-expanded state may be missing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2968-2980: Ensure aria-expanded is set on all FAQ triggers in HTML markup as fallback, or run state initialization synchronously before any event listeners are attached

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons use aria-pressed but roving tabindex may conflict
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates() function manages aria-pressed on filter tabs, but the createRovingTabindex controller also manages tabIndex. The interaction between roving tabindex and aria-pressed states is not coordinated, potentially causing confusion for screen reader users about which tab is both focused and active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2294-2304: Ensure _rovingTabs.setActive() is called in conjunction with aria-pressed updates, or document that aria-pressed indicates selection while tabIndex indicates focus

issuesFound.title: View toggle buttons set aria-pressed but initial state may not match DOM
issuesFound.explanation: The _onViewToggle() function sets aria-pressed on view mode buttons, but the initial state is read from localStorage without verifying the DOM reflects this state on page load. If CSS hides/shows elements differently than the stored preference, aria-pressed may not match visible state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2335-2343: Add initial aria-pressed synchronization in productFilter.init() after restoring saved view mode from storage

issuesFound.title: Carousel play/pause button aria-pressed not initialized on load
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel play() and pause() functions update aria-pressed on _playBtn, but the initial state is not set during init() before auto-play begins. If auto-play starts immediately, the button may show aria-pressed="false" while the carousel is actually playing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3290-3303: Add setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'true'); in the init() function if auto-play is enabled

issuesFound.title: Carousel slide aria-hidden set but tabindex management incomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The goTo() function sets aria-hidden and tabindex on slides, but when slides are dynamically added or removed, the tabindex values may not be recalculated. This can leave orphaned tabindex="0" on hidden slides.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3235-3244: Add a refresh function that recalculates tabindex for all slides when the carousel is modified, and call it after any DOM changes

issuesFound.title: File upload zone role="button" conflicts with contained input element
issuesFound.explanation: The fileUploadField bind() function sets role="button" on the drop zone container which also contains a native input type="file". This creates an interactive element inside another interactive element, which is forbidden by ARIA. Screen readers may not properly expose the file input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3699-3703: Remove role="button" from the zone element. Instead, ensure the contained input is properly labeled and the zone acts as a visual enhancement only, with click forwarding to the input

issuesFound.title: File upload zone tabindex on container may trap keyboard users
issuesFound.explanation: Setting tabindex="0" on the upload zone container creates an additional tab stop before the actual file input. Keyboard users must tab twice to reach the functional input, and the container itself has no keyboard activation beyond triggering the input click.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3702: Remove tabindex from the zone container. Keep tabindex only on the native input element. Use CSS to style the container as clickable without making it separately focusable

issuesFound.title: SUPPORTS.dom reference is undefined throughout the codebase
issuesFound.explanation: The createFocusTrap function references SUPPORTS.dom at lines 1218 and 1283, but this property does not exist in the SUPPORTS object defined at lines 348-408. This will cause a runtime error, preventing the focus trap from activating in modals and drawers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1218: Change SUPPORTS.dom to true (DOM is always available when JS runs). Line 1283: Same fix

issuesFound.title: srUtils.annotateExternalLinks references undefined STRINGS key
issuesFound.explanation: The annotateExternalLinks function references STRINGS.opensInNewTab at line 1970, but this key does not exist in the STRINGS object defined at lines 447-520. This will cause undefined to be appended to external link labels announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1970: Change to STRINGS.labels.opensInNewTab || ', opens in new tab' (add this key to STRINGS.labels)

issuesFound.title: liveRegion double-buffer may announce empty strings during buffer swap
issuesFound.explanation: The _announce function clears the inactive buffer before writing to the active buffer. If the requestAnimationFrame callback is delayed, there is a brief moment where both buffers are empty, which some screen readers may announce as "blank" or "no content".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1125-1162: Write to the active buffer first, then clear the inactive buffer after a small delay, ensuring content is always present in at least one buffer

issuesFound.title: formAutosave restores values but may not trigger validation updates
issuesFound.explanation: The formAutosave restore() function emits an 'input' event after restoring values, but inlineValidator may not be bound yet when restore() is called during init. This means character counters and validation states may not update to reflect the restored values.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3524-3526: Call restore() after inlineValidator.bind() is complete, or add a setTimeout to defer the input event emission until after all validators are attached

issuesFound.title: contactForm error summary links use ensureId but may create duplicate IDs
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderErrorSummary function calls srUtils.ensureId(field, 'contact-field') for each error link. If multiple fields lack IDs, ensureId will generate unique IDs, but if this function is called multiple times during validation cycles, IDs may be regenerated inconsistently.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3876: Ensure IDs are generated once during form init and stored, rather than generating them during each error summary render

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard progress bar aria-valuetext may be too verbose
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateProgress function sets aria-valuetext to "Step X of Y" which is announced on every step change. Combined with the liveRegion.announce() call at line 3189, screen reader users hear the step information twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3161: Remove aria-valuetext from the progress bar and rely solely on the liveRegion.announce() call, or vice versa—not both

issuesFound.title: navKeyboardShortcuts does not check for inert or aria-disabled elements
issuesFound.explanation: The _onKeydown handler in navKeyboardShortcuts checks for input/textarea/select tags but does not check if the target element has inert attribute or aria-disabled="true". Keyboard shortcuts may fire when they should be suppressed inside disabled form sections.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2623-2628: Add check for e.target.closest('[inert]') || e.target.getAttribute('aria-disabled') === 'true' before allowing shortcuts to fire

issuesFound.title: siteSearch status element created dynamically may not be announced immediately
issuesFound.explanation: The _statusEl is created and appended during siteSearch.init(), but if the search input is focused before init completes, there is no aria-describedby target. Users may begin typing before the status region exists to receive result count announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2189-2196: Create the status element in HTML markup with the expected ID, or ensure siteSearch.init() runs before the search input is reachable via tab order

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters announces via emit but not via liveRegion
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function emits a custom 'filter:applied' event with count information, but this event is not connected to the liveRegion.announce() system. Screen reader users will not hear how many products match their filter unless a separate listener is added.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2247: Add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`); after emit() call

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput uses aria-live="assertive" for all messages
issuesFound.explanation: The _onApply function sets aria-live="assertive" on the status element for both success and error messages. Error messages warrant assertive politeness, but success messages should use polite to avoid interrupting the user's current task unnecessarily.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2585-2588: Set aria-live="polite" for valid results and aria-live="assertive" only for invalid/error results

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel dot navigation buttons lack visible text alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The _buildDots function creates buttons with textContent set to the slide number (e.g., "1", "2", "3"), but these numbers alone do not convey what the button does. Screen reader users hear "1" but not "Go to testimonial 1".
issuesFound.fix: Line 3273-3284: Keep the numeric textContent for visual users but ensure aria-label includes action text: aria-label={`Go to testimonial ${i + 1} of ${_slides.length}`}

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader hide/show events not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The stickyHeader module emits custom 'header:hide' and 'header:show' events but these are not connected to liveRegion announcements. Users who rely on screen readers are not informed when the header disappears or reappears during scroll, which may be disorienting if focus is near the top of the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1590-1609: Add liveRegion.announce('Header hidden') and liveRegion.announce('Header visible') in the respective hide/show branches

issuesFound.title: mobileNav overlay aria-hidden not managed during transitions
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open() function removes hidden attribute from the overlay and adds CSS visible class, but does not manage aria-hidden. During the CSS transition, the overlay may be visually fading in but still has aria-hidden="true" (or no aria-hidden at all), causing inconsistent AT experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1467-1475: Set aria-hidden="false" when removing hidden attribute, and set aria-hidden="true" when adding hidden attribute in the close function

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu hover open does not match click open for aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _onHover function opens dropdowns on mouseenter without updating aria-expanded, while _onTriggerClick does update it. Keyboard users and screen reader users get different state information than mouse users, violating the principle of equivalent experiences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1740-1751: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); in the _onHover 'enter' branch and setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); in the 'leave' branch

issuesFound.title: createRovingTabindex does not announce selection changes
issuesFound.explanation: The setActive function in createRovingTabindex emits a 'roving:select' custom event but does not announce the selection change to screen readers via liveRegion. Users navigating with arrow keys receive no confirmation of which item is now active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1348-1371: Add liveRegion.announce(`${item.textContent || 'Item'} selected`); after the emit() call in setActive function

issuesFound.title: skipLinkManager removes tabindex on blur which may cause focus loss
issuesFound.explanation: The _ensureFocusable function removes tabindex after blur to keep the target out of natural tab order. However, if a user activates the skip link again quickly, the element may not have tabindex when focus is attempted, causing focus to be lost or go to an unexpected location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1426-1431: Keep tabindex="-1" permanently on skip targets. This does not affect tab order (negative tabindex is not in tab sequence) but ensures the element is always programmatically focusable

issuesFound.title: scrollIntoView behavior may conflict with user motion preferences
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollIntoView function checks SUPPORTS.prefersReducedMotion which is evaluated once at script load. If the user changes their OS motion preference while the page is open, the scroll behavior will not adapt. The prefersReducedMotion() function at line 839 should be used instead.
issuesFound.fix: Line 739-747: Replace SUPPORTS.prefersReducedMotion with prefersReducedMotion() function call to check current preference at time of scroll

issuesFound.title: animateNumber does not announce final value to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The animateNumber function animates numeric counters but never announces the final value to screen readers. Users who cannot see the animation will not know what number was reached. The statCounterAnimator module attempts to set aria-label but this happens after animation completes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 897-928: Add liveRegion.announce(`Counter reached ${to}`); in the onComplete callback, or ensure the element has aria-live="polite" before animation begins

issuesFound.title: performanceMonitor sends vitals but no user-accessible report
issuesFound.explanation: The performanceMonitor module collects and sends Core Web Vitals to analytics but provides no mechanism for users to access this performance data. Users with cognitive disabilities who need to know if the page is performing poorly have no way to check.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4374-4421: Add an optional debug mode that displays vitals in a visually-hidden live region, or provide a keyboard shortcut to announce current performance metrics

issuesFound.title: errorReporter batches errors but does not notify users of failures
issuesFound.explanation: The errorReporter module catches and reports JavaScript errors to analytics but never informs the user that an error occurred. If a critical function fails silently, users may continue interacting with a broken interface without knowing why features are not working.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4435-4488: Add optional user notification for critical errors via liveRegion.announceAssertive('A feature is not working correctly. Please refresh the page.') for errors that affect core functionality

issuesFound.title: ContactForm Name Input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput references an error ID ('contact-name-error') via aria-describedby, but this ID is never rendered because the parent FormGroup does not receive the errorId/errorMessage props required to display the ErrorMessage component. Screen readers announce a relationship to a non-existent element.

  Line 1395: <TextInput id="contact-name" ... aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: ContactForm Email Input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput references an error ID ('contact-email-error') via aria-describedby, but this ID is never rendered because the parent FormGroup does not receive the errorId/errorMessage props. This breaks the programmatic association between the field and its error message.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm Subject Input broken aria-describedby reference

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input references non-existent error ID
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput element includes an aria-describedby attribute that conditionally references 'newsletter-email-error'. However, the parent FormGroup does not pass the errorId or errorMessage props required to render the ErrorMessage component with that ID. When validation fails, screen readers announce an error ID that does not exist in the DOM.

issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput element for 'Full name' includes an aria-describedby attribute referencing 'contact-name-error'. The parent FormGroup uses the prop name 'error' instead of 'errorMessage' and omits 'errorId', so the ErrorMessage component is never rendered. This creates a broken ARIA reference when validation fails.

issuesFound.title: Contact form email input references non-existent error ID

The parent FormGroup fails to render the corresponding ErrorMessage element due to incorrect prop names ('error' vs 'errorMessage') and missing 'errorId', resulting in a dangling ARIA reference.

issuesFound.title: Notification dismiss functionality broken due to context property mismatch
issuesFound.explanation: The `NotificationContainer` component destructures `dismiss` from `NotificationContext`, but the provider supplies `removeNotification`. This causes `dismiss` to be undefined, making the dismiss buttons in `NotificationToast` throw an error or fail silently when clicked. Users cannot close notifications, which may obscure content or repeat announcements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1353: const { notifications, removeNotification } = useContext(NotificationContext);
  Line 1362: <NotificationToast key={n.id} notification={n} onDismiss={removeNotification} />

issuesFound.title: Notification container aria-live attribute suppresses child announcements
issuesFound.explanation: The `NotificationContainer` div has `aria-live="off"`, which instructs assistive technology to ignore changes within this region. This conflicts with the child `NotificationToast` elements that use `role="alert"` or `role="status"` to announce messages. The container's setting may prevent notifications from being announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1356: <div className="notification-container" aria-label="Notifications">

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute placed on overlay instead of dialog
issuesFound.explanation: The `aria-modal="true"` attribute is applied to the `.modal-overlay` div, which does not have a role. According to ARIA spec, `aria-modal` should be on the element with `role="dialog"` to indicate that content outside the dialog is inert. Placing it on the overlay is invalid and may not be recognized by assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1258: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>
  Line 1260: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} ...

Consequently, the Demo Modal defined in `LuminaryPage` has no trigger button to open it, rendering the feature inaccessible and unreachable for all users.

issuesFound.fix: Line 1185: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing button is part of a toggle group tracking the value state but lacks aria-pressed. The selection state is not communicated to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1193: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

issuesFound.title: HeroSection section missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <section> element for the hero area lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby. While it contains an h1, the landmark itself is not labelled for skip navigation or region lists.
issuesFound.fix: Line 825: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <section> element for statistics lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby. It contains a visually hidden h2, but the section tag itself must reference it to be properly named.
issuesFound.fix: Line 905: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link indicating the current page uses a current prop for styling but lacks aria-current="page". Screen readers do not announce this link as the current location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 565: <a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation link indicating the current page uses a current prop for styling but lacks aria-current="page". Screen readers do not announce this link as the current location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 685: <a href={item.href} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The <article> element representing a pricing plan contains a heading with an id but does not reference it via aria-labelledby. The card lacks an accessible name in region lists.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1235: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm button missing aria-busy due to prop mismatch
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button passes a loading prop, but the Button component expects isLoading to set aria-busy. Consequently, aria-busy is undefined, and loading state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1465: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm button missing aria-busy due to prop mismatch
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button passes a loading prop, but the Button component expects isLoading to set aria-busy. Consequently, aria-busy is undefined, and loading state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1595: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting}>

issuesFound.title: Skip navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav> element wrapping the skip links lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby. When multiple nav landmarks exist on a page (e.g., main nav, footer nav), each must have a unique accessible name to allow users to distinguish them.
issuesFound.fix: Line 435: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: Star rating decorative icons not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The <span> elements rendering the star characters (★/☆) inside StarRating are not hidden. Since the container already has an aria-label describing the rating, these decorative icons create redundant noise for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 465: <span key={i} className={filled ? 'star star--filled' : 'star star--empty'} aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy for loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an isLoading prop but does not pass aria-busy={isLoading} to the underlying <button> element. Assistive technologies need this state to announce that the button is temporarily unavailable due to processing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 490: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} ...

issuesFound.title: Required field marker announces bare asterisk to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The <span> containing the asterisk (*) in FormGroup does not have aria-hidden="true". Screen readers will announce "asterisk" or "star" unnecessarily, as the "required" state is already conveyed via the hidden text "(required)" and the input's required attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 530: *

issuesFound.title: TextInput component does not propagate aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop for styling but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the <input> element. This prevents assistive technology from announcing the field as invalid when an error occurs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 553: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={...}

issuesFound.title: Textarea component does not propagate aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop for styling but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the <textarea> element. This prevents assistive technology from announcing the field as invalid when an error occurs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 573: <textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={...}

issuesFound.title: Select component does not propagate aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an invalid prop for styling but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the <select> element. This prevents assistive technology from announcing the field as invalid when an error occurs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 603: <select id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={...}

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown decorative chevron missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The <span> containing the chevron character (▲/▼) is decorative but lacks aria-hidden="true". It may be announced as text, creating redundancy alongside the button label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 642: {isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}

issuesFound.fix: Line 665: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none but only provides outline-offset without a visible outline width, style, or colour. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus because the outline is completely removed with no replacement box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn min-height 32px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets a min-height of 32px, which falls between the 24px minimum and 44px recommended target sizes. Without sufficient padding to ensure a 44px total height, users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating the button.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn-sm min-height 36px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm variant sets min-height: 36px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. This may make the button difficult to activate for users with tremors or limited precision.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width/height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px, well below the 44px recommended touch target size. This small target may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate accurately.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] width/height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has a fixed width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. This makes the checkbox difficult or impossible for some users to activate reliably.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a min-height 24px
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px, meeting the absolute minimum but falling short of the 44px recommended target size. This may cause difficulty for users with motor impairments when trying to activate the links.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible on screen rather than hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to assistive technologies. This breaks the intended functionality of providing content only to screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn transform transition
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (for the :active state), but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this motion for users who have requested reduced motion. This may cause vestibular disorders or discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6
issuesFound.explanation: Heading elements use letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) which resolves to -0.025em. Negative letter-spacing reduces legibility, particularly for users with dyslexia or low vision, by crowding characters together.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .section-header h2
issuesFound.explanation: The section header h2 uses letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) (-0.025em), which negatively impacts readability by reducing the space between characters.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .hero-content h1
issuesFound.explanation: The hero h1 uses letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tighter) (-0.05em), which significantly reduces character spacing and impairs readability for users with cognitive or visual disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .features-section .section-header h2

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px, which does not render a visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot perceive which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix: Line 373: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, failing to provide a visible focus indicator for buttons when navigated via keyboard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 671: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size with insufficient padding on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 36px (within 24–43px range) but vertical padding is only 6px (var(--space-1-5)), which is less than the 8px required to ensure the 44px recommended touch target size is met.
issuesFound.fix: Line 751: .btn-sm { min-height: 36px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size with insufficient padding on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px (24–43px range) with zero padding, failing to meet the 44px recommended touch target size for pointer inputs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 963: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2303: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have min-height: 24px with no padding specified in the rule, providing less than the recommended 44px touch target area without sufficient compensation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2330: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:active selector applies transform: translateY(1px) spatial movement without a prefers-reduced-motion media query override, which may cause vestibular issues for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 675: .btn:active { transform: translateY(1px); }
  @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card hover effect uses transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) without a reduced motion override, unlike the base .pricing-card:hover which is properly guarded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1908: .pricing-card--featured:hover { box-shadow: var(--shadow-2xl); transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01); }
  @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only adjusts outline-offset, which does not create a visible focus ring. This removes the focus indicator for keyboard users on all interactive elements, making it impossible to perceive which element has keyboard focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus indicator for keyboard users on all button elements, violating WCAG 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below 44px recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width of 28px and height of 28px with zero padding, resulting in a touch target of only 28×28 pixels. This is below the 44×44px recommended size and may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width of 20px and height of 20px, which is below the 24×24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to select the control.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px without sufficient padding to reach the recommended 44px touch target size. This may make the links difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which fails to visually hide the content. This makes screen-reader-only text visible to sighted users, breaking the visual design and potentially exposing content that should be hidden.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class applies a transition on the transform property without a prefers-reduced-motion media query override. Users who have enabled reduced motion preferences may still experience the sliding animation, which can cause vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The blockquote element uses var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa (light grey) on a white background. This results in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for section header paragraph
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p selector uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1. This fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (4.5:1).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for hero lead text
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-lead class uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1. This fails the WCAG AA minimum contrast requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for logo bar label
issuesFound.explanation: The .logo-bar-label class uses var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.1:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for footer address
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand address selector uses var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) on var(--colour-neutral-900) (#0f172a) background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 4.0:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for footer bottom text

issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible alternative. High contrast mode users lose focus indication entirely.
issuesFound.fix: Line 407-409: `@media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus-visible state removes the default outline and provides only an outline-offset with no visible ring, shadow, or border. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons are focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 573: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link's focus-visible state removes the outline without providing a box-shadow or border alternative. Keyboard users lose focus indication on the site logo.
issuesFound.fix: Line 681: `.header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the focus outline without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when this critical control is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 702: `.nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links remove the focus outline with no box-shadow or border alternative. Keyboard users navigating submenus cannot see which link is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 757: `[id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the focus outline without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see when the search button is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 804: `#site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the focus outline without a visible replacement. Keyboard users lose focus indication on these links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 821: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove the focus outline without providing a box-shadow or border alternative. Keyboard users cannot see when these card links are focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1002: `.feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove the focus outline without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which accordion item is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1089: `.faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible, select:focus-visible, textarea:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative in the focus-visible state. While these elements have a :focus box-shadow, the focus-visible rule explicitly removes it without replacement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1167: `.form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs remove the focus outline without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see when checkboxes are focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1170: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users lose focus indication on these links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1194: `.footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-neutral-0); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove the focus outline without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when footer links are focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1204: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-neutral-0); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users lose focus indication on these links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1215: `.social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-neutral-0); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets a minimum height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this small target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 640: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon button sets both minimum width and height to 20px, below the 24px minimum. This creates a touch target that is too small for reliable activation by users with motor disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 652: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs are sized to 20px by 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users with motor impairments may struggle to select these checkboxes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1173: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width and height of 28px with zero padding, resulting in a touch target of 28px. While this meets the 24px minimum, it falls below the 44px recommended size without sufficient padding to compensate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 800: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Screen reader only text will be visible on screen, breaking the visual layout and potentially confusing sighted users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 434-444: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button element includes transform in its transition property, which can cause motion animation. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion) override to disable this transform transition for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 575: `.btn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: var(--space-2); min-height: 32px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-5); font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); line-height: var(--line-height-tight); text-align: center; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap; cursor: pointer; user-select: none; border: 2px solid transparent; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); vertical-align: middle; position: relative; isolation: isolate; }` (remove transform from transition)

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image includes transform in its transition property. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block exists but is empty, failing to disable the transform transition for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 939-942: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing to -0.2em, which reduces the space between words. This negative spacing can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities by making text harder to parse.
issuesFound.fix: Line 125: `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links use color var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against a background of var(--colour-neutral-900) (#555555), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.9:1. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1207: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-200); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer address text
issuesFound.explanation: The footer address uses color var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) against a background of #555555, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.8:1. This is below the 4.5:1 required for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1194: `.footer-brand address { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-300); line-height: var(--line-height-loose); font-style: normal; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on stats section labels
issuesFound.explanation: Stat labels use rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) (30% opacity white) against a gradient background including #5588bb. The resulting contrast ratio is approximately 1.5:1, well below the 4.5:1 required for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 121: `.stat-label { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85); line-height: var(--line-height-snug); max-width: 160px; text-align: center; margin: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links inherit text-decoration: none from the global anchor rule and only change color on hover without adding an underline, border-bottom, or other non-colour visual differentiator. Users with colour vision deficiencies cannot distinguish these links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1212: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }`

fpTitles: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
Insufficient colour contrast on stats section labels
Link underline removed with no alternative on footer navigation

issuesFound.title: Scroll-to-top button lacks keyboard accessibility safeguards
issuesFound.explanation: The code queries an element via `SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn` and binds a click handler, but does not ensure the element is a native `<button>`, nor does it add `role="button"`, `tabindex="0"`, or keyboard event handlers for Enter/Space. If the selector matches a `<div>` or other non-interactive element, the control is completely inaccessible to keyboard and assistive technology users.

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison trigger missing aria-controls reference
issuesFound.explanation: The `setComparisonTableExpanded` function toggles the visibility of the comparison table and calls `setExpanded(trigger, expanded)` to update the trigger button's `aria-expanded` state. However, neither function sets `aria-controls` on the trigger to reference the table element (`[data-pricing-comparison]`). Screen reader users cannot programmatically determine which content region is controlled by the trigger.

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison toggle aria-expanded not initialized at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing.init()` function initializes the billing period and highlights the recommended plan, but does not call `setComparisonTableExpanded` or otherwise initialize `aria-expanded="false"` on the `[data-expand-comparison]` trigger if the comparison table is collapsed by default (the typical state). This leaves the trigger without a declared state on initial page load, violating the requirement that the state of collapsible widgets must be indicated immediately.

fpTitles: Scroll-to-top button lacks keyboard accessibility safeguards
Pricing comparison trigger missing aria-controls reference
Pricing comparison toggle aria-expanded not initialized at page load

issuesFound.title: Contact form first name input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form input fields for first name, last name, email, company, and team size are selected via SELECTORS but the code does not programmatically set the autocomplete attribute on these elements. This prevents browsers from correctly identifying the input purpose for autofill and assistive technologies, failing WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix: Line 296: After selecting contactFirstName, add `setAttr(_contactFirstName, 'autocomplete', 'given-name');` Line 297: `setAttr(_contactLastName, 'autocomplete', 'family-name');` Line 298: `setAttr(_contactEmail, 'autocomplete', 'email');` Line 299: `setAttr(_contactCompany, 'autocomplete', 'organization');` Line 300: `setAttr(_contactTeamSize, 'autocomplete', 'off');` (or appropriate value)

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input element (id="newsletter-email") is selected in the newsletterForm module but does not receive an autocomplete attribute. This prevents proper identification of the input purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 290: After selecting newsletterEmail, add `setAttr(_emailEl, 'autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button is selected via SELECTORS.navToggle and initialized in mobileNav.init. While the code sets aria-expanded and aria-controls, it does not ensure the button has an accessible name (aria-label, aria-labelledby, or visible text). Screen reader users will hear only "button" or "collapsed/expanded" without knowing what the button controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1040 (in mobileNav.init): After selecting _trigger, add `if (!_trigger.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_trigger.textContent.trim()) { setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.openMenu); }` and update the label in open/close functions.

issuesFound.title: Scroll to top button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button is selected via SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn. The code manages its hidden state and aria-hidden attribute, but never ensures it has an accessible name. When visible, screen readers will not announce its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1240 (in scrollToTop.init): After selecting _btn, add `if (!_btn.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_btn.textContent.trim()) { setAttr(_btn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.scrollToTop); }`

issuesFound.title: Product filter tab buttons missing aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons control the visibility of the product grid but do not have aria-controls attributes linking them to the grid container. This prevents assistive technologies from understanding the relationship between the tabs and the controlled content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1840 (in productFilter._syncTabAriaStates or init): After selecting _gridEl and ensuring it has an id, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', _gridEl.id);` for each filter tab button.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) update the pricing display but lack aria-controls attributes pointing to the pricing cards container or the specific price elements they update.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2100 (in pricing._syncToggleState): Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', 'pricing-cards-container');` (or the actual id of the pricing grid element).

issuesFound.title: File upload field validation not reflecting aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: When file validation fails in _addFile (size or type), the code displays an error message via liveRegion but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the file input element itself. Screen reader users may not know the field is in an error state when navigating back to it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2850 (in fileUploadField._addFile validation block): Add `setAttr(binding.inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and `setAttr(binding.inputEl, 'aria-errormessage', errorRow.id);` where errorRow is the created error element.

issuesFound.title: Search clear button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search clear button is selected via SELECTORS.searchClear (or cfg.clearSelector) but the code does not ensure it has an accessible name. If the button contains only an icon (×), screen readers will not announce its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1620 (in siteSearch.init): After selecting _clearBtn, add `if (!_clearBtn.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_clearBtn.textContent.trim()) { setAttr(_clearBtn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.clearSearch); }`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing accessible name verification
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function attaches ARIA attributes to trigger buttons but does not verify or ensure the triggers have accessible names. If the HTML contains buttons with only icons or no text content, they will be unnamed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1100 (in dropdownMenu.register): After selecting the trigger, add `if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-label') && !trigger.textContent.trim()) { setAttr(trigger, 'aria-label', trigger.getAttribute('aria-haspopup') === 'true' ? 'Menu' : 'Toggle'); }`

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel navigation buttons missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The previous and next buttons in the testimonials carousel control the slide track but do not have aria-controls attributes linking them to the carousel track or the active slide.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2450 (in testimonialsCarousel.init): After selecting _prevBtn and _nextBtn, add `setAttr(_prevBtn, 'aria-controls', _trackEl.id);` and `setAttr(_nextBtn, 'aria-controls', _trackEl.id);`

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard step panels using aria-label instead of aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard step panels are given aria-label with the step title text, but they should use aria-labelledby referencing the actual heading element id to ensure the accessible name updates if the heading content changes dynamically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2700 (in multiStepWizard._parseSteps or _goToStep): Instead of `step.panelEl.setAttribute('aria-label', step.title);`, use `const titleId = srUtils.ensureId(qs('h2, h3', step.panelEl), 'step-title'); setAttr(step.panelEl, 'aria-labelledby', titleId);`

issuesFound.title: Comparison table header cells missing scope attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The planComparisonHighlighter module manipulates the comparison table but does not ensure the table header cells (th) have scope attributes (col or row). Without scope, screen readers may not correctly associate headers with data cells in complex tables.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2200 (in planComparisonHighlighter.init): After selecting the table, call `srUtils.makeTableAccessible(_tableEl);` which applies scope="col" to header row cells and scope="row" to first column header cells.

issuesFound.title: Wizard progress bar not announcing step changes via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the wizard advances to a new step, the progress bar updates visually and its aria-valuenow changes, but there is no accompanying live region announcement for the progress percentage or step change, which may not be detected by all screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2650 (in multiStepWizard._updateProgress): After updating the progress bar attributes, add `liveRegion.announce(\`Step \${stepIndex + 1} of \${_steps.length}, \${Math.round(percent)}% complete\`);`

issuesFound.title: Active filter chips remove buttons missing aria-describedby reference to filter name
issuesFound.explanation: The remove buttons in activeFilterChips have aria-label "Remove filter: {label}" which is good, but they could additionally use aria-describedby to reference the chip text for redundancy. However, the current implementation is acceptable.
issuesFound.fix: (Optional) Line 1950: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-describedby', chipLabelId);` where chipLabelId is the id of the span containing the filter text.

issuesFound.title: Form error summary links may reference non-existent ids if fields lack ids
issuesFound.explanation: In contactForm._renderErrorSummary, the error links use `srUtils.ensureId(field, 'contact-field')` to generate hrefs. If the field already has an id, it uses that; otherwise it generates one. However, if the field is not in the DOM or the id generation fails, the link target may be invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2400: Ensure all fields have explicit ids set in the HTML or via JS before creating error links. Add `srUtils.ensureId(field, 'contact-field');` before creating the link href.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle switch (when not a native button) is given role="switch" and tabindex="0", but in the init function, aria-checked is only set in _syncToggleState which is called after setPeriod. If the initialization fails or the element is not a button, aria-checked might not be set initially.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2080 (in pricing.init): After ensuring role and tabindex, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion search input missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ search input (when present) controls the visibility of FAQ items but does not have an aria-controls attribute linking it to the FAQ list container.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2550 (in faqAccordion.init): After selecting _searchInputEl, add `setAttr(_searchInputEl, 'aria-controls', _containerEl.id);`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer acts as a modal dialog (traps focus, closes on outside click, blocks interaction with the rest of the page) but does not have the aria-modal="true" attribute. Screen reader users may not realize they are in a modal context and may attempt to navigate outside the drawer.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1765 (in open function): Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-modal', 'true');` after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`
    Line 1789 (in close function): Add `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-modal');` before or alongside the class removal.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When a user opens or closes a dropdown menu (via click or keyboard), the visible state of the panel changes but this is not announced via the live region. Users relying on screen readers may not be aware that content has appeared or disappeared, especially since focus moves into the panel on open but the context change is not explicitly announced.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1890 (in _openInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` after `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
    Line 1905 (in _closeInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` after `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications auto-dismiss without pause mechanism
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notifications automatically dismiss after a configured duration (dur) using setTimeout. There is no mechanism for users to pause, stop, or extend this timer (e.g., on hover or focus), violating WCAG 2.2.2 (Pause, Stop, Hide). Users with cognitive disabilities or those using screen readers may not have sufficient time to read the notification.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent dialog does not return focus on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the cookie consent banner (role="dialog") is closed via the Accept or Decline buttons, focus is not returned to the element that triggered the banner (or the previously focused element). This causes focus loss and forces screen reader users to navigate from the top of the page.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer may remain in accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is hidden by removing CSS classes. If the associated CSS uses off-screen positioning (e.g., transform: translateX(-100%)) rather than display: none, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree. The JavaScript does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer when closed to ensure it is hidden from assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1789 (in close function): Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` 
    Line 1765 (in open function): Add `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');` (or set to 'false')

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle buttons may lack accessible name verification
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons are queried via `[data-period]` selectors. The JavaScript updates aria-pressed but does not verify or ensure that the buttons have accessible names (either text content or aria-label). If the HTML contains icon-only buttons without labels, they will be inaccessible.

issuesFound.title: Scroll-to-top button may lack accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button is queried via SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn. The JavaScript assumes it exists but does not verify it has an accessible name. If the button contains only an icon (e.g., an arrow SVG) without text or aria-label, screen reader users will not know its purpose.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel navigation buttons may lack accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The previous and next buttons are queried via `[data-carousel-prev]` and `[data-carousel-next]`. The JavaScript adds click handlers but does not ensure these buttons have accessible names. If they are icon-only, they will be inaccessible.

issuesFound.title: Wizard form navigation buttons may lack accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The previous and next buttons in the multi-step wizard are queried via `[data-wizard-prev]` and `[data-wizard-next]`. The JavaScript does not verify these buttons have accessible text or labels.

issuesFound.title: Search clear button may lack accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search clear button is queried via SELECTORS.searchClear. The JavaScript toggles visibility but does not ensure the button has an accessible name.

issuesFound.title: Product filter view mode buttons may lack accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The view mode toggle buttons (grid/list) are queried via `[data-view-mode]`. The JavaScript sets aria-pressed but does not ensure the buttons have accessible names.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers may lack accessible name verification
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ triggers are queried and parsed. The JavaScript ensures they have role="button" and tabindex="0" if not native buttons, but does not verify they have accessible names (text content or aria-label).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers may lack accessible name verification
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown triggers are queried and ensured to have aria-haspopup and aria-expanded, but accessible names are not verified.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger may lack accessible name verification
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button is queried but its accessible name is not verified by the JavaScript.

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher buttons may lack accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: Theme toggle buttons are queried via `[data-theme-toggle]`. The JavaScript cycles through themes but does not verify the buttons have accessible names.

issuesFound.title: Contact form submit button may lack accessible name during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: When the form submits, the button text is changed to 'Sending…' but if the button initially had no accessible name (icon only), the text change might not be sufficient, or if the loading text is not descriptive. However, the main issue is that the button is disabled during loading but the focus remains on it; screen readers will announce the disabled state and new text.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form submit button may lack accessible name during loading
issuesFound.explanation: Similar to contact form, the button text changes to 'Subscribing…' but initial accessible name is not verified.

issuesFound.title: File upload zone live region status not cleared
issuesFound.explanation: The status element for file uploads has aria-live but is not cleared after announcements, potentially causing old messages to be re-read.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3350: After announcing, clear after a delay:
    ```javascript
    setTimeout(() => { if (binding.statusEl) binding.statusEl.textContent = ''; }, 1000);
    ```

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison table expand button missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The function uses setExpanded helper which sets aria-expanded, but if the table starts expanded on page load, the initial aria-expanded might not be set by JavaScript (relies on HTML).

issuesFound.title: Stat counters animate without reduced motion check for screen reader verbosity
issuesFound.explanation: While the code checks prefersReducedMotion for the visual animation, it updates the DOM text content frequently during animation. Screen readers may announce each update if the user is focused on or near the element. The aria-label is set only on complete.
