issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the mobile navigation drawer and locks scroll, but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1850: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;` inside the `open` function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() omits aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened, the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute is not set to "true". The companion `_closeInstance` sets it to "false", so the state is only communicated when closing, creating an asymmetric and inaccessible experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2100: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of `_openInstance` after `inst.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() omits aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding an FAQ answer panel does not update the triggering button’s `aria-expanded` attribute to "true". The `close()` function likewise fails to set it to "false" explicitly (it is only set during initialisation), leaving assistive technologies unaware of the current expansion state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4500: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside `open()`. Similarly, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside `close()`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() omits aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked invalid, the function updates `aria-describedby` to link the error message but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field itself. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2200: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside `markInvalid()` before `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap declared but never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a `_trap` variable for focus management but never initialises it with `createFocusTrap()` or calls `_trap.activate()` when opening the drawer. Consequently, keyboard focus is not trapped within the mobile menu, allowing users to tab into background content while the modal-like drawer is open.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to move focus into drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the navigation drawer opens, focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the first focusable element inside the drawer (or the drawer itself). Keyboard and screen-reader users must manually navigate into the newly revealed content.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() assumes drawer has id for aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The code attempts to set `aria-controls` on the trigger only if `_drawer.id` exists, but it does not ensure the drawer element has an `id` attribute. If the HTML lacks an id, the relationship between trigger and drawer is never established, breaking accessibility for screen-reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1800: Before the aria-controls check, add:
    ```
    if (!_drawer.id) _drawer.id = `mobile-nav-drawer-${shortId()}`;
    ```

fpTitles: mobileNav.open() fails to move focus into drawer
mobileNav.init() assumes drawer has id for aria-controls

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the function adds CSS classes and emits an event but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot convey the expanded state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1554: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_lockScroll();` and before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves assistive technologies unaware that the menu is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1682: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding a FAQ accordion item animates the panel and updates state, but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3127: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid fails to set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The function associates an error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot identify the field as invalid, violating WCAG 4.1.2 and 3.3.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1084: Insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu lacks live-region announcement for state changes
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes visible content but provides no auditory feedback via an aria-live region. Screen reader users are unaware when a dropdown appears or disappears.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1682 (after setAttr): Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Dropdown opened');` 
       Line 1698 (after setAttr): Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Dropdown closed');`

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader hides/shows without live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The header hides on scroll-down and reappears on scroll-up, but these state changes are not announced to screen readers. Users navigating by keyboard may be disoriented when the header disappears.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1786: After `addClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden);` insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.headerHidden || 'Header hidden');` 
       Line 1791: After `removeClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden);` insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.headerShown || 'Header shown');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer but never updates the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation panel is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1220: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the `_open = true;` assignment within the open() function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The companion _closeInstance() correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1975: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');` in the _openInstance function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates() function toggles visual active classes on filter tab buttons but does not set aria-pressed to indicate the pressed state to assistive technologies. Since these buttons act as a toggle group (only one active at a time), they must communicate their state via aria-pressed or aria-selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2185: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() utility function links the error message via aria-describedby but omits setting aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Consequently, screen readers will not announce the field as invalid despite the presence of an error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 802: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the markInvalid function before or after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap is never instantiated or activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable intended for focus trapping but never instantiates it using createFocusTrap(), and consequently never calls activate() when the menu opens. Keyboard users can tab out of the open navigation drawer into the background page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1225: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: true });` before activating. Line 1235: Add `_trap.activate();` inside the open() function after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not save or restore focus on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function fails to save document.activeElement to _savedFocus before moving focus, and the close() function attempts to return focus to _savedFocus which is always null. Focus is lost when the navigation closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1222: Add `_savedFocus = document.activeElement;` at the start of the open() function. Ensure close() uses this reference to restore focus.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not move focus to navigation drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the drawer or the first focusable element within it. Screen reader users may not realize the navigation has opened or be able to navigate its contents efficiently.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1238: Add `_drawer.focus({ preventScroll: true });` or focus the first focusable child inside _drawer after opening.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger may lack aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The init() function sets aria-controls on the trigger only if the drawer element already has an id attribute. If the drawer lacks an id, the trigger will not have aria-controls, breaking the programmatic association between the trigger and the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1283: Ensure the drawer has an ID before setting aria-controls: `if (!_drawer.id) _drawer.id = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav');` then proceed to set aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField uses deprecated aria-dropeffect attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload zone sets aria-dropeffect to "copy" or "none". This attribute is deprecated in ARIA 1.1 and may not be supported by assistive technologies. Modern drag-and-drop implementations should manage state via live regions or other accessible patterns.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3290: Remove `setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-dropeffect', 'copy');` and Line 3300: Remove `setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-dropeffect', 'none');`. Replace with visual feedback and live region announcements if necessary.

issuesFound.title: pricing highlightPlan accumulates aria-label text on repeated calls
issuesFound.explanation: The highlightPlan() function appends "(Recommended)" to the existing aria-label every time it is called (e.g., when switching billing periods). This causes the label to grow indefinitely (e.g., "Pro (Recommended) (Recommended) (Recommended)").
issuesFound.fix: Line 2380: Instead of appending, set the label explicitly: `plan.el.setAttribute('aria-label', \`${plan.el.getAttribute('data-plan-name') || plan.id} ${STRINGS.recommendedPlan || '(Recommended)'}\`);` or clear the previous suffix before appending.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer trigger button does not have its aria-expanded attribute set to "false" when the menu closes. Screen reader users cannot perceive that the menu is collapsed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button lacks aria-expanded="false" on initial page load, so screen readers do not know the menu is initially collapsed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button lacks aria-controls pointing to the drawer element's ID, failing to establish the programmatic relationship required by the ARIA disclosure pattern.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer, allowing keyboard users to tab into background content behind the modal-like overlay.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing focus return to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it, disorienting keyboard and screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion trigger buttons do not set aria-expanded="true" when expanding an answer panel, violating the WAI-ARIA accordion pattern and preventing screen readers from announcing the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation marks a field as invalid, it does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clearing
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation clears an error, it does not remove aria-invalid or set it to "false" on the input element, leaving screen readers with stale invalid state information.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator._renderResult missing aria-invalid update
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validator renders error messages but relies solely on srUtils.markInvalid which is also missing aria-invalid support. The field remains without aria-invalid="true" when invalid.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the updated result count and filtered content are not announced to screen readers via a live region, leaving blind users unaware that the content has changed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes visible content but does not announce the state change via a live region, so screen reader users may not notice the menu appeared or disappeared.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates visible prices dynamically but does not announce the change or new prices via a live region, leaving screen reader users unaware of the price update.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons indicate active state via CSS classes but lack aria-pressed (or aria-current) attributes, so screen readers cannot convey which filter is active.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel.init missing initial aria-pressed on play button
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel play/pause button does not initialize aria-pressed to reflect the initial auto-play state, so screen readers cannot convey whether the carousel is currently playing or paused.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element's ID, failing to establish the programmatic relationship required by the disclosure pattern.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens, the code references a focus trap variable (_trap) but never initializes or activates it, allowing keyboard focus to escape into the background page content.

issuesFound.title: Product filter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons (showing active/inactive state) but the _syncTabAriaStates() function only toggles CSS classes without setting aria-pressed, so screen readers cannot convey which filter is active.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function emits a custom event but does not write to the live region to announce that the navigation has closed, leaving screen reader users unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2491 (before the closing brace): Add `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); }`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu reveals new content but does not trigger a live region announcement, so screen reader users may not realize the menu has appeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2612 (after `emit(...)`): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu removes content from view without announcing the change via a live region, potentially confusing screen reader users who may still think the menu is available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2629 (after `emit(...)`): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing updates the displayed prices but does not announce the change via the live region, so screen reader users are not informed of the price update.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3570 (after `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', ...)`): Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'month' ? STRINGS.pricingMonthly : STRINGS.pricingAnnual);`

issuesFound.title: Scroll to top button activation not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking the scroll-to-top button smoothly scrolls the page but does not announce the action completion to screen readers, leaving users uncertain if the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2860 (after `setTimeout(...)`): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');` inside the setTimeout or immediately after it.

issuesFound.title: Form validation markValid does not clear aria-invalid

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function hides the drawer by removing a CSS class, but if the CSS positions the drawer off-screen rather than using display:none, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree. The code does not set aria-hidden="true" when closing.

issuesFound.title: productFilter changes not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions manage dropdown panel visibility but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger buttons. Assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded/collapsed state of the menus.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1330: In _openInstance, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` before the emit call.
  Line 1342: In _closeInstance, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion triggers missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open and close functions animate panel height and toggle classes, but fail to set aria-expanded on the question buttons. Screen readers cannot convey whether an answer is visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2165: In open function, add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `item.open = true;`.
  Line 2185: In close function, add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle is assigned role="switch" but _syncToggleState only toggles CSS classes. It never updates aria-checked, so screen readers cannot announce whether the switch is in the "year" or "month" state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1986: In _syncToggleState, after `toggleClass(_toggleTrack, CSS.checked, isYear);` add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', isYear);`

issuesFound.title: Form validation functions not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility adds error descriptions via aria-describedby but omits aria-invalid="true", while markValid does not clear the attribute. Screen readers cannot identify which fields contain errors.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1070: In markInvalid, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after addDescribedBy.
  Line 1078: In markValid, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` (or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`) after updating aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button controls the mobile drawer but lacks an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer’s id, preventing screen readers from identifying the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1248 (init): After `_drawer = qs(SELECTORS.navDrawer || SELECTORS.primaryNav);` add `if (_trigger && _drawer) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer. Keyboard users can tab out of the menu into the underlying page content. The _trap variable is declared but never activated in the open function.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1194 (open): After `_open = true;` and the drawer visibility setup, add:
  `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });`
  `_trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: productFilter not announcing filter results
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count updates but the change is not announced via a live region. Screen reader users are unaware that filtering has completed or how many items are now visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1560 (applyFilters): After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);` add:
  `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.filterResultsCount || '{count} products found').replace('{count}', String(totalMatches)));`

issuesFound.title: pricing module not announcing period changes
issuesFound.explanation: Toggling the billing period updates prices visually but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users may not realise the prices have switched between monthly and annual.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1946 (setPeriod): After the plan cards update loop (after `planDataParser.getAll().forEach(...)`), add:
  `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to {period} billing').replace('{period}', period));`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion not announcing expand/collapse
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items changes the visible content without announcing the state change to screen readers, leaving users unaware that the answer has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2165 (open): Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.announce.faqOpened || '{title} answer expanded').replace('{title}', item.title));` before the emit call.
  Line 2185 (close): Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.announce.faqClosed || '{title} answer collapsed').replace('{title}', item.title));` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav not announcing open/close state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens and closes without announcing its state via the live region, so screen reader users cannot tell when the menu is visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1194 (open): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);` after `_open = true;`.
  Line 1210 (close): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);` at the start of the function (before `_open = false;`).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller updates panel visibility but fails to set aria-expanded="true" or "false" on the trigger button when opening or closing dropdowns, leaving screen readers unaware of the state change.

issuesFound.title: Form validation missing aria-invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are marked as invalid, the function adds an error description via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true", preventing screen readers from announcing the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In markInvalid, after addDescribedBy(field, errorEl); add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Form validation not clearing aria-invalid on success
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing validation errors, the function removes the aria-describedby reference but fails to set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute, incorrectly leaving the field marked as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In markValid, after the if block handling aria-describedby removal, add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads, so screen readers do not know the initial collapsed state of the drawer.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are registered without initializing aria-expanded="false", leaving screen readers unaware that the panels are initially collapsed on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In register, after const panelId = srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel'); add: setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Product filter result count not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filtering products, the result count badge is updated visually but not announced to screen readers via a live region, leaving blind users unaware of how many products match their criteria.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In _updateCountBadge, after setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-label', ...); add: liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the price updates are visible but not announced via a live region, so screen reader users miss the content change.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In setPeriod, after planDataParser.getAll().forEach(plan => _updatePlanCard(plan, period)); add: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer is not announced to screen readers, so users receive no confirmation when the navigation state changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In open(), after _lockScroll(); add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened'); Line: In close(), after _unlockScroll(); add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown open and close actions emit custom events but are not announced via live regions, preventing screen reader users from knowing when dropdown content becomes available or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In _openInstance, before emit, add: liveRegion.announce(`${inst.trigger.textContent} expanded`); Line: In _closeInstance, before emit, add: liveRegion.announce(`${inst.trigger.textContent} collapsed`);

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) act as mutually exclusive toggles but do not use aria-pressed to indicate the active state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In _syncToggleState, inside _periodBtns.forEach after toggleClass, add: setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap not activated
issuesFound.explanation: Although a focus trap is created for the mobile navigation drawer, it is never activated when the menu opens, allowing keyboard focus to escape to background page elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In open(), after _lockScroll(); add: if (_trap) _trap.activate();

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button lacks aria-controls pointing to the drawer element, preventing screen readers from understanding which element is controlled by the button.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility via CSS classes but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen readers will not announce the expanded or collapsed state to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1790: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` at the start of the open function.
    Line 1812: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` at the start of the close function.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions show and hide the dropdown panel but fail to set aria-expanded="true" or aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button (inst.trigger). Assistive technologies cannot track the menu state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1890: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` in _openInstance after `inst.open = true;`.
    Line 1900: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` in _closeInstance after `if (!inst.open) return;`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function sets item.open = true and animates the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger). The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an asymmetric state where opening is not communicated to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3500: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `item.open = true;` in the open function.

issuesFound.title: Site search combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._open and _close functions toggle the suggestion listbox visibility but do not update aria-expanded on the search input (_inputEl). The input has aria-haspopup="listbox" but lacks the expanded state, preventing screen readers from announcing when suggestions are available.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2800: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` at the start of _open.
    Line 2810: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` at the start of _close.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator not setting aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function marks fields as invalid by adding CSS classes and error messages but calls srUtils.markInvalid, which only manages aria-describedby and does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers will not identify the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4050: Insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);` in the else block (invalid case) before or after the existing srUtils.markInvalid call.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator not clearing aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When inlineValidator._renderResult validates a field successfully, it calls srUtils.markValid but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false". The field remains marked as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4040: Insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` in the if (result.valid) block.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function sets up event listeners on the trigger button but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" for the closed state on page load. Screen readers encounter the button without state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1850: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after confirming _trigger exists in the init function.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.init and register functions set up dropdowns but do not initialise aria-expanded="false" on the trigger buttons when the page loads. The panel is hidden via the hidden attribute, but the trigger lacks the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1970: Insert `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` in the register function after setting aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: Site search input missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init function configures the input as a combobox with aria-controls and aria-haspopup but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" for the closed suggestion list on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2850: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after setting other ARIA attributes in the init function.

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the visible product cards and emits a custom event but does not write to an aria-live region. Screen readers do not announce the filter result count or category change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3100: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : \`Showing \${s.activeCategory} products\`);` after the filter animation completes.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel but does not announce the state change via the liveRegion. Users are not informed that content has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3500: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened ? STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) : \`\${item.question} expanded\`);` at the end of the open function.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.close function collapses the answer panel but does not announce the state change via the liveRegion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3520: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed ? STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question) : \`\${item.question} collapsed\`);` at the end of the close function.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function accepts an announceToSR parameter but never uses it to write to the liveRegion. Opening the navigation drawer is not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1790: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` at the end of the open function.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function does not announce the navigation closure via the liveRegion, leaving users unaware that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1812: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` at the end of the close function.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates displayed prices and emits an event but does not announce the billing period change via the liveRegion. Users do not hear confirmation of the price toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3300: Insert `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);` at the end of setPeriod.

issuesFound.title: Product view mode toggle lacks keyboard accessibility
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.init uses delegate to bind click events to '[data-view-mode]' elements but does not ensure these elements have role="button", tabindex="0", or keyboard handlers for Enter/Space. If these are divs or spans, they are inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3150: Add keyboard support by inserting after the delegate call: `qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(btn => { if (btn.tagName !== 'BUTTON') { setAttr(btn, 'role', 'button'); setAttr(btn, 'tabindex', '0'); on(btn, 'keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); btn.click(); } }); } });`

issuesFound.title: Plan comparison headers lack keyboard accessibility
issuesFound.explanation: The planComparisonHighlighter.init uses delegate for click and mouseenter on '[data-plan-head]' elements but does not add role="button", tabindex, or keyboard handlers. If these are table headers or cells, they are not keyboard operable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3400: After the delegate calls, add: `qsa('[data-plan-head]').forEach(h => { if (h.tagName !== 'BUTTON' && h.tagName !== 'A') { setAttr(h, 'role', 'button'); setAttr(h, 'tabindex', '0'); on(h, 'keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); h.click(); } }); } });`

issuesFound.title: Contact form full name input missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The contactForm binds validation to the full_name field but does not set the autocomplete attribute. The field clearly collects personal name data (selector '[name="full_name"]') but lacks autocomplete="name".
issuesFound.fix: Line 4300: In the init function where CONTACT_FIELDS are processed, insert `if (field.name === 'full_name') field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');` when binding the field.

issuesFound.title: Contact form email input missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The contactForm binds validation to the email field (selector '[name="email"]') but does not set autocomplete="email", preventing browsers from autofilling the user's email address.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4300: Insert `if (field.name === 'email') field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');` when processing the email field in init.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email input missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletterForm.init function binds validation to the email input but does not set autocomplete="email" on _emailEl, preventing proper autofill.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4200: Insert `_emailEl.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');` after confirming _emailEl exists in the init function.

issuesFound.title: Wizard full name input missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The multiStepWizard STEP_FIELDS configuration includes '[name="wizard_name"]' but the init function does not set autocomplete="name" on this field when binding validation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4400: In the init function where STEP_FIELDS are processed, insert `if (cfg.selector.includes('name')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');` or specifically for the wizard_name field.

issuesFound.title: Wizard email input missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The multiStepWizard STEP_FIELDS includes '[name="wizard_email"]' but the init function does not set autocomplete="email" on this field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4400: Insert `if (cfg.selector.includes('email')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');` when processing the wizard_email field.

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog aria-modal attribute misplaced on overlay
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay backdrop div instead of the dialog element with role="dialog". issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative stars not hidden from AT

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron not hidden from AT

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk (*) indicating a required field is not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce "star" which may be confusing. The "(required)" text is already provided via VisuallyHidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 800: *

issuesFound.title: Button loading state missing aria-busy

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button controls a sub-menu but lacks aria-expanded to communicate its open/closed state to assistive technology. Screen-reader users cannot tell whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1156: <button ... aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-controls={menuId} ...>

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button contains only an emoji icon (☀️/🌙/💻) and lacks an aria-label prop, leaving it without an accessible name. The LABELS variable is defined but never passed to the component.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1282: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">{ICONS[theme]}</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken error message reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input has aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error" but FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop, so the ErrorMessage element is never rendered with that ID. This creates a broken ARIA reference and prevents error announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1850: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.error} ...> and ensure TextInput aria-describedby matches.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm inputs broken error message references
issuesFound.explanation: Each input references an error ID (e.g., 'contact-name-error') via aria-describedby, but FormGroup receives the `error` prop instead of `errorId` and `errorMessage`, so the ErrorMessage elements are never rendered. This results in broken ARIA references.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1940: Pass errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.error} to FormGroup, and similarly for other fields.
